claims.claim: Website security issues (missing headers, policies) create active litigation risk
Page speed at 45/100 hurts both UX and Google ranking signals
Domain Rating of 53 with 858 referring domains represents significant SEO equity worth protecting during platform migrations
Migrating 100+ blog posts from Beaver Builder to Elementor risks losing indexed pages and disrupting internal link equity
Approximately 90% of Overhead Door's web form traffic is residential
Spam bot form submissions use nearly identical wording, indicating automated activity
Seamless's previous website generated 202 monthly visits yielding only ~6 engagements, most sales solicitations rather than genuine leads
WordPress outperforms Shopify for SEO; Google demonstrably rewards WordPress sites over Shopify
Asymmetric's site holds 227 organic keywords and ~5,900 monthly visitors (declining but recoverable)
Citrus America's current website is beautiful but fails to convert visitors into leads
Citrus America's current site leads with machines rather than the problem being solved for retailers
Full website management (hosting, SEO, ongoing optimization) costs ~$500/quarter
Google Ads Quality Score is influenced by landing page relevance and experience
Site migrations may silently drop SEO metadata (e.g., meta descriptions)
Organic traffic improvements from meta description completion typically lag by days to weeks as search engines re-crawl
Missing or misconfigured SSL certificates cause browser 'Not Secure' warnings that deter visitors before any messaging can work
Mixed content (combination of https:// and http:// resources) causes browser security warnings even with valid SSL certificate
Complimentary Elementor rebuild scope is limited to building existing designs, not creating new ones
Kinsta automatic backups are not always visible from WordPress admin panel; must check Kinsta dashboard directly
Kinsta sites may use different plugins and configurations than WP Engine-hosted sites; caching plugins may conflict with Kinsta's native cache layer
Asymmetric should avoid editing websites it does not manage; if something breaks, there is no clean path to fix it without coordinating with external party
Long, unbroken walls of text in blog posts reduce scannability and likely cause reader bounce, especially for informational content
Redirecting an outdated domain preserves SEO value and inbound traffic compared to taking it offline
Placing H1 text directly over hero images requires dark overlay for legibility, which obscures photos and crops faces
Removing H1 from hero image and placing it below allows full-color, properly framed photos without overlay
Contact form submissions can silently fail due to WordPress mail sending (wp_mail) being blocked by hosting providers; SMTP plugin configuration is often the fix
Fieldwell™ trademark applies to the brand name 'Fieldwell' alone, not the full phrase 'Fieldwell CBD'
Take9 product line name is written as one word (no space, no hyphen)
Divi theme does not natively support custom Slick Slider elements; custom sliders injected via functions.php cannot be edited through Divi's visual editor
Staging sites are expected to be ready for interactive review within 2–3 weeks when copy and project content are the primary bottlenecks
Removing navigation from landing pages and keeping only a Contact Us anchor link prevents prospects from clicking away before converting
Visual product galleries serve as implicit social proof without requiring written testimonials
Websites that conflate two distinct brands under one web presence create confusion for prospects and generate unqualified inbound calls
Contact forms are preferable to raw email links because form submissions are trackable as discrete conversion events in Google Analytics and Google Tag Manager
Individual user logins enable personalized event tracking and auto-filled registration forms compared to shared company logins
State pages contribute to SEO by creating indexable, keyword-rich pages targeting regional search terms and expanding the site's keyword footprint
Warranty language must distinguish between contractor-issued workmanship warranties and manufacturer warranties to avoid overpromising
Missing security headers (CSP, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy) are treated as a quality signal by Google's crawlers and ranking algorithms
Bot traffic overload can be mitigated by updating Cloudflare firewall rules to block known bot signatures and suspicious traffic patterns
Database query counts of 600+ indicate a serious backend problem, typically caused by plugin conflicts, unoptimized queries, or bot-driven traffic spikes
Ahrefs health scores below 80 should be prioritized for remediation; target 80+ for all managed sites
A 99% Ahrefs health score is near-perfect and difficult to achieve, reflecting the absence of crawl errors, broken links, and orphaned pages
Structured FAQ sections formatted as direct Q&A enable AI models to easily extract and cite content
Out-of-the-box platforms are faster and cheaper to build but impose hard ceilings on customization that can be expensive and disruptive to cross mid-project
Custom React builds have the highest initial investment and highest ongoing care-and-feeding cost because every update, bug fix, and feature addition requires developer time
A shared Google Doc tracker with clearly defined status columns (Ready for Review / Final Review) creates a single source of truth for website review workflows
Accumulated log files and bot traffic can cause sites to degrade from 380 MB to require cleanup, with load times improving from 6s+ to under 2s after remediation
Bot traffic running card-testing scams can be mitigated by removing vulnerable products and implementing CAPTCHA on checkout endpoints
Dynamic copy blocks allow shared page templates to maintain consistent layout while enabling page-specific content without global propagation of edits
Standard WordPress contact forms are not HIPAA compliant without a Business Associate Agreement (BAA)
SEO-focused pages require 700–1,000 words of original copy to signal topical depth to search engines
Static PDF catalogs receive zero SEO value and cannot be indexed by search engines
Shopify's templated structure does not allow custom code for graphics, display rotations, and interactive elements
A separate domain carries no meaningful SEO downside for campaigns driven entirely by email and LinkedIn (not PPC)
Homepage copy that reads acceptably in a document often feels overwhelming once rendered in actual page layout
Service-based e-commerce sites should capture customer intent in storefront and handle variant selection in the service workflow, not the browse experience
Newly licensed therapists in Madison, WI are actively struggling to find supervisors, representing a high-intent, underserved local search niche
State-specific landing pages with local CallRail numbers improve perceived local credibility for out-of-state prospects
Testimonials should display quote text first, followed by name and attribution, with explicit quotation marks
Hero images that crop on mobile viewports reduce visual impact and user engagement
Phone numbers placed only in hero images are inaccessible as users scroll, reducing call conversion opportunities
Vague CTAs like 'Select your state' create user confusion about expected outcomes
Interactive questionnaire tools reduce inbound call volume to admissions teams by pre-qualifying prospects
Automated Playwright testing can identify mobile responsiveness issues across all screen sizes
Low contrast text (light purple on champagne background) fails accessibility thresholds for users with low vision
Gender-neutral language ('partners' instead of 'bride/groom') accommodates approximately 20% of the wedding market (same-sex and non-binary couples)
Date validation rules prevent illogical event scheduling (e.g., rehearsal dinner after wedding date)
Two-column project grid layouts maximize visual impact of photography compared to three-column layouts
SEO-driven navigation labels ('Dementia Care') confuse users even when the keyword appears on dedicated pages
High-traffic pages with zero conversions indicate critical UX or performance issues
Mobile page speed score of 80 correlates with visitor drop-off and zero conversions on service pages
Sticky headers disappear when scrolling past slanted section dividers due to z-index/CSS stacking context conflicts
Dynamic logo resize on scroll prevents header from obscuring page content while maintaining navigation accessibility
Form submissions routed to shared inboxes create delays in client notification and response
Post-submission page scroll-to-top behavior leaves users unaware that confirmation messages exist below the fold
Custom form confirmation messages may not display if form builder defaults override custom settings
Service page variant selectors (e.g., 'Face' vs. 'Face + Neck') allow users to self-select relevant pricing and details without page reloads
Expandable accordion sections reduce cognitive load by showing only variant-specific content
Service pricing varies significantly by vehicle (e.g., LED headlight installation: 15 min on Civic vs. 2 hrs on F-150), making direct booking CTAs inappropriate
Canbus decoders (~$50/pair) are required on many vehicles (Jeep, Dodge) to prevent error codes when LEDs are installed
LED headlight manufacturer claims of '50,000 hour' lifespan are misleading; 3-year warranty is the practical customer commitment
Amazon-style product page layouts (short description above fold, thumbnails on left) improve usability and reduce drop-off
Displaying all resource logos at equal size prevents unintended visual hierarchy that creates political friction among partner organizations
Position (top-left, top-right) can signal priority among logos without creating visual size hierarchy
ABM landing pages targeting specific verticals with vertical-specific copy and imagery outperform generic landing pages
Patented child-resistant technology is a differentiator relevant specifically to cannabis accounts and should be surfaced selectively
Domain rank of 6/100 signals low search engine trust and makes organic ranking unlikely until improved
Websites optimized for appearance rather than conversion routinely fail to convert visitors into leads
Paid traffic routed to homepages instead of dedicated landing pages produces near-zero conversion rates
Generic CTA labels like 'Learn More' hide content rather than drive action
Inactive legacy social media accounts linked from website footers create brand confusion and may outrank active accounts in search results
Gravity Forms is preferred over WP Forms for WordPress builds due to reliability, extensibility, and developer familiarity
Forms should live where data needs to go; if a CRM has capable native forms and client is committed to platform, use CRM forms instead of WordPress plugins
Some hosting providers block outbound mail from PHP by default, causing form submission failures at email notification step
Website launches require real-time monitoring and cannot be delegated to offshore team members due to timezone constraints
Full Elementor rebuilds are faster and less risky than in-place fixes when a site is built on unsupported builders or incompatible plugins
Placeholder events on a live website damage credibility and should be removed before launch
Two-sided marketplace platforms benefit from separate audience paths rather than conflated homepages
WordPress templates are preferable to custom JavaScript builds when launch deadlines are active
Contact forms must preserve per-page source tracking when rolled out site-wide
Long-form content (2000+ words) outperforms for informational queries in B2B SEO
claims.note: Referenced in system prompt as example but not present in batch

claims.claim: Sending paid traffic to homepage instead of dedicated landing pages results in zero conversions
claims.evidence: Aviary spent $800 with ~385 visitors and zero conversions; all traffic routed to homepage

claims.claim: Dedicated landing pages with single conversion goal and matched messaging outperform generic pages
Generic CTAs like 'Learn More' hide content rather than drive action
Free trial is a significant conversion asset when prominently featured
High-commitment forms at bottom of funnel create friction before visitor is convinced of value
PDF-based content is invisible to search engines and reduces SEO value
Converting PDFs to individual webpages makes content crawlable and indexable
State-specific landing pages targeting geographic variants expand keyword surface area
claims.evidence: BluePoint organic traffic doubled; site tracking 124 keywords with organic keyword count growing

claims.claim: Sticky header with persistent CTA ensures conversion option remains visible as users scroll
Multiple phone numbers on landing page dilute tracking and create decision fatigue
Website edits going live with errors indicates QA process gaps
claims.evidence: Broken phone numbers and garbled copy found on BluePoint homepage

claims.claim: Incognito-window testing catches cache-dependent issues like dynamic number swapping
Contact page is highest-leverage page on service business website
Service businesses receiving organic traffic can lose leads if no clear contact path exists
claims.evidence: A New Dawn Therapy received 3+ inquiries per week despite incomplete site because visitors emailed footer address

claims.claim: Gravity Forms + Zapier is standard integration pattern for WordPress landing pages
React/Cursor/Vercel/Supabase stack is roughly one-tenth the size of equivalent WordPress build
WordPress plugin workarounds for non-standard requirements lead to costly friction
Help text beneath form fields reduces user confusion about required fields
Sticky language selector dropdown is more persistent than browser-level translation prompts
Facebook Messenger chat widget captures after-hours leads from users unable to call
claims.evidence: AHS estimated 2-3 chat contacts per month; justified given high job value

claims.claim: Missing address in website footer caused a customer to purchase from competitor instead
claims.context: Overhead Door Madison customer intended to visit business, Googled address, ended up at 'Madison Overhead' competitor instead — confirmed lost sale

claims.claim: Address + map embed in footer is a low-effort, high-protection fix for local service businesses competing with similarly-named competitors
Interior hero images outperform exterior/aerial shots for senior living facilities
claims.context: Prospective residents/families need to imagine themselves in the space; aerial drone views are emotionally inert

claims.claim: Inline form success messages cause user confusion; dedicated Thank You page redirect is the recommended pattern
claims.context: Users perceive inline refresh as failure/loop rather than success; dedicated page provides unambiguous confirmation

claims.claim: Dedicated Thank You pages cannot reliably host 'Add to Calendar' widgets that pull event data from originating page
WordPress sites suffer from recurring breakage caused by plugin update conflicts
Next.js enables SEO tasks (meta descriptions, alt text, title tags) to be executed as single commands across entire site in ~15 minutes vs. hours in WordPress
Named client testimonials on homepage create a prospecting list for competitors
claims.evidence: Competitor (Indiana Lifestone Company) used published testimonials to contact and win away all named clients

claims.claim: BluePoint ATM state pages have missing mid-page phone number, creating visibility gap as users scroll
claims.rationale: Goal is visual effect of local presence, not necessarily more calls

claims.claim: Hero images that disappear on mobile devices undermine responsive design
claims.context: BluePoint ATM reverse ATM graphic disappears at mobile breakpoints

claims.claim: Vague CTAs on interactive elements reduce clarity; descriptive text improves user understanding
claims.example: 'Select your state' → 'Click on your state to see our local service offerings'

claims.claim: Campaign landing pages should follow a 10-section structure: Hero, Goal/Progress, Facts Strip, About, Impact, Community Support, Why Now, Vision, Investment Breakdown, Beneficiary Experience
Facts & stats on landing pages should be displayed horizontally, not vertically
Architectural renderings are ideal for 'Our Vision' section of capital campaign landing pages
Off-brand embedded forms (e.g., Further form) create disjointed user experience at conversion points
Form fills are the preferred lead capture method over phone calls for Cordwainer
Evergreen job descriptions (standing roles like Nurses, CNAs) on careers pages attract talent even when no specific opening is posted
WordPress form plugins often fail to send confirmation emails to submitters
claims.context: Further form test inquiry did not trigger confirmation email

claims.claim: Website form submissions for Overhead Door Madison dropped to near-zero for approximately two weeks in January 2026
January is typically a slow period for garage door service and installation
claims.context: Seasonal factor but does not fully explain complete halt

claims.claim: Anonymous client quotes on project-level pages preserve social proof without creating prospecting list
claims.pattern_applies_to: relationship-driven B2B (custom fabrication, luxury construction, bespoke manufacturing)

claims.claim: Didion Masa Narepa product page hero carousel has rendering issues: disappearing slider, black box artifacts on certain screen sizes
'Request a Sample' CTA wording is acceptable for B2B product pages when samples are genuinely free
FarmWise facility serves four-state region (Wisconsin, Iowa, Illinois, Minnesota), not just Doudlah Farms
claims.context: Facility provides cleaning, packing, white-label services to regional organic farmers

claims.claim: Owning processing facility directly supports 'Tested Clean' brand promise by eliminating ambiguity about product identity
Construction progress logs with photos every 2-3 weeks keep stakeholders engaged in facility development
FinWellU homepage required complete content replacement and structural redesign before February 17 launch
FinWellU trademark symbol should be ® (registered) not ™ (trademark) sitewide
Three-image footer block (Overview/You Thrive/Retire U) should appear at bottom of every page as consistent cross-navigation element
Pre-populated registration forms for logged-in users reduce friction and ensure platform holds current contact data
Sticky header logo sizing should fill blue bar evenly rather than sitting closer to top
Termly auto-updates privacy policy based on applicable laws, removing need for manual maintenance
VCEDC website had three mobile responsiveness bugs blocking production launch: hero image overflow, social icon stacking, button misalignment
Fresh developer review is more effective than original developer self-debugging for identifying blind spots
Browser drag-to-narrow does not always match real device behavior for responsiveness testing
QR codes linking to manufacturer pages have zero SEO benefit for store's own domain
Custom catalog pages (Option B QR strategy) build keyword-rich content improving organic search rankings
Press release should link to dedicated product page, not homepage, to provide complete product experience
Product page launch should be delayed until spec sheet, NSF certificate, and dedicated page are all ready
JBF Concrete has only Instagram as active social media presence; no YouTube or LinkedIn
Landscape-oriented, high-resolution images are preferred for concrete landscaping website layouts
React + Cursor + Vercel + Supabase stack is appropriate for projects requiring custom booking logic, complex B2B portals, or precise UI behavior
React sites are roughly one-tenth the size of equivalent WordPress builds
Cursor AI code editor requires precise, technically-informed prompts; vague instructions produce poor results
Supabase can handle scale that WordPress native database cannot (millions of records, instant queries)
Bluepoint website database was reduced 20% (42MB → 34MB) by removing orphan data and stale options
claims.metric: 8.5 MB orphan data removed, 18,000 artifact rows deleted, 28 stale options cleared

claims.claim: Bluepoint website SSL rating improved from Flexible to A+ after hardening
Bluepoint website meta description coverage increased from 72% to 98%
Bluepoint website achieved Ahrefs Health Score of 100% with zero crawl errors
Alt text coverage of ~89-90% is practical ceiling due to non-image file types (PDFs, documents)
Didion website is returning HTTP 500 errors; root cause unknown
Didion switched from Square to PayPal in August but website payment integration was never updated
claims.impact: Golf outing sponsorship page cannot process payments

claims.claim: Square plugin may be contributing to or compounding website 500 errors
Navigation menus with hover-activated submenus reduce redundancy and improve user clarity compared to flat, multi-level displays
Dedicated landing pages optimized for specific ad keywords and audience segments improve conversion rates compared to sending traffic to homepages
Tour-to-move-in conversion rate for memory care facilities is exceptionally high (7 of 8 visitors convert)
Blog pages with category filters can serve the same purpose as separate blog instances while preserving SEO value
Stock photography that does not reflect the client's actual geography undermines trust and brand authenticity
Site replacement over an existing live domain requires 301 redirects, automation re-establishment, and post-launch crawl validation to avoid broken links and lost integrations
Monthly website maintenance audits (image alt text, plugin updates, form performance, analytics validation) prevent technical debt accumulation
Custom careers pages on client websites outperform external job board redirects in terms of SEO value and brand control
Phone number visibility on interior pages reduces friction in lead generation flows for phone-preferring users
Two-step written approval process (copy approval + staging page approval) prevents unapproved or incomplete website changes from going live
Asymmetric has two developers capable of building 1–2 new websites per month in addition to maintaining existing client sites
Website development capacity is not yet priced into budget projections despite representing a meaningful revenue opportunity
Slow page load times on L'Amarie Beauty staging site are caused by volume of content loaded without filtering
Category filter implementation is expected to improve both perceived and actual load speed on L'Amarie Beauty site
Maintaining both old and new hosting services simultaneously during DNS migration is cheaper than risking downtime
Quarra website development shifted from email-only coordination to twice-weekly meetings to accelerate progress
PaperTube landing pages prioritize household brand logos over niche-relevant brands for credibility signaling
Quaritalia website has been in development for over three months but cannot launch due to missing client-provided content assets
FinWellU standard onboarding and event setup requests have a 3–4 business day turnaround SLA
FinWellU urgent requests (e.g., mass layoff notifications) require same-day turnaround
AviaryAI previously tested multi-product navigation and found it diluted the core message and hurt conversion
BluePoint ATM first batch of 10 state pages was ready to push live as of 2026-02-25
Icon-based case study format was replaced with narrative format due to inconsistency and poor handling of multi-service projects
B2B website projects often extend beyond initial estimates due to scope complexity
Citrus America dealer landing page was 95% complete as of December 2024 with only form dropdown finalization and two UI fixes remaining
Board member profiles without photos or bios should display name and title only, with 'Learn More' links removed entirely
BluePoint ATM state pages must have 'Industry Expertise' section updated to match renamed categories from main website redesign before going live
Landing page quality is one of the most impactful variables in Google Ads performance
High impressions and clicks with low conversions signals landing page is the bottleneck, not ads
Homepages used as ad destinations are almost always suboptimal; dedicated landing pages consistently outperform
Multi-step dialogue-form design produces richer lead data and better qualification than single-page static forms
Form submissions are preferable to phone calls as primary CTA for B2B service firms because they create written records and enable async review
VCEDC homepage had 7-8 second average visit time indicating users couldn't quickly find what they needed
Tight-cropped hero images read cleaner and more professional at full-width scale than wide shots with background context
Sending Google Ads traffic directly to third-party booking tools prevents analytics ownership, remarketing, and A/B testing
JavaScript websites are faster and lighter weight than WordPress
Preserving existing URLs during a full website rebuild prevents loss of SEO equity
NRCA membership signals adherence to industry standards and is a meaningful trust signal for commercial roofing buyers
Manufacturer-backed labor warranties differentiate from contractor-only warranties and serve as third-party endorsement
Restoration-first philosophy differentiates SBSWI from large commercial roofing competitors who default to tear-off
Interactive questionnaires reduce friction and improve lead quality by pre-qualifying visitors before contact
Microsite strategy with dense cross-linking back to main site avoids splitting SEO authority while establishing local presence
Redirect strategy preserves legal and SEO equity while allowing new sub-brands to grow
Navigation labels should describe what visitors will find, not internal organizational vocabulary
Homepage should function as visual sitemap mapping directly to main navigation menu items
AI tools can rapidly audit landing page quality and surface specific improvement priorities
Specialty page duplication pattern with template swapping keeps website builds manageable and ensures consistent structure
H1 must contain primary target keyword and be placed in hero section for SEO effectiveness
FAQ sections are high-leverage for keyword density and ranking in AI-generated snippets
Inaccurate website photography (showing amenities that don't exist at specific locations) creates liability exposure and erodes trust with prospective customers
Inherited WordPress sites accumulate technical debt: redundant plugins, non-standard form systems, missing SEO fundamentals, and orphaned content
Generic CTAs like 'Learn More' and 'Send Message' fail to convert bottom-of-funnel paid ad traffic; action-oriented labels like 'Schedule a Call' or 'Get a Free Consultation' perform better
Landing pages without explanation of post-submission outcomes (e.g., 'someone will call within 24 hours') experience higher form abandonment
Hosting video assets on third-party platforms like Vimeo via embed prevents page speed degradation compared to self-hosting video files
Product overview pages containing more detailed content than individual product pages create counterintuitive user experience where deeper navigation yields less information
A divided content ownership model (agency provides structural outlines, client writes copy) ensures technical accuracy while maintaining agency control over architecture
Three distinct specialty page templates accommodate variation in content flow better than a single restrictive template
Low search volume for 'social anxiety therapy' as standalone query justifies folding it into broader 'Anxiety Therapy' page with supplemental blog content for top-of-funnel traffic
Lighter green header with subtle 'V' background watermark was preferred over darker forest green (perceived as too similar to previous site) and lime/gradient options (too bright)
YouTube branding (logo, black bars) should be suppressed on embedded videos in video grids to maintain clean design
Website rebuild is preferable to incremental optimization when external vendor dependency prevents timely updates and service offerings are inadequately represented
Sites with 87% of traffic concentrated on homepage indicate poor internal linking and low discoverability of service pages

patterns.pattern: Website security and compliance issues (ADA accessibility, security headers, legal policies) are foundational blockers that must be resolved before design/content work
Form design improvements driven by direct customer feedback reveal missing options (emergency vs. standard, new vs. repair, payment method differentiation)
Platform migration decisions are driven by SEO equity preservation and content volume; staying on existing platform is preferred when domain authority is high
Website rebuilds are timed strategically around business cycles (slower seasons) to concentrate effort without disrupting revenue
Form integrations with external systems (KIT, ConvertKit, Google Forms) are critical post-launch configuration tasks requiring client system access
Hero image replacement from generic/exterior shots to interior/lifestyle photography improves emotional connection and conversion
Navigation menu ordering reflects business priorities; secondary offerings (respite care, sold-out sponsorships) should be de-emphasized or hidden
Website redesigns shift from product/service showcase to problem-solution framing, leading with customer pain point rather than offering
Accessibility and readability are prioritized over design elegance; bold fonts and muted colors outperform thin/saturated alternatives
Contact Us pages are highest-priority builds and should be completed early, even before rest of site, to capture organic traffic during development
Foundational site infrastructure (blog sections, location page templates, footer standardization) is built during paused periods to enable faster activation when client bandwidth increases
CTA button proliferation creates visual clutter and reduces conversion; strategic reduction of button count increases prominence of remaining CTAs
User behavior data (heat maps, analytics) should inform design decisions over stakeholder opinion when conflict arises
Certifications and memberships pages build trust and credibility; should be featured prominently and integrated across multiple entry points (About Us, dedicated page)
Event/calendar pages benefit from clear separation of upcoming vs. past events with differentiated CTAs (Register vs. Read More)
Website quality directly affects paid ad ROI through Quality Score, landing page relevance, and conversion rates
Post-launch website audits are necessary to catch SEO metadata gaps, broken elements, and UX issues that were not caught during initial build
Mobile display bugs (cropped images, cut-off text, hidden headers) are common issues requiring developer attention post-launch
Hero image and staff photo presentation issues (cropped faces, poor framing, overlays) recur across multiple client sites and impact user experience and trust
CTA placement and clarity are frequently missing or suboptimal on pages with conversion intent, reducing lead generation
Service/product pages benefit from dropdown navigation and clear topical hierarchy to improve both UX and SEO
Audience-specific messaging and form logic (e.g., distinguishing between different user types) requires explicit copy and conditional form fields
Video content on landing pages increases engagement compared to static photo slideshows
Duplicate pages and redundant content occur when early drafts are not cleaned up after final versions are approved
Brand naming conventions (trademark usage, product line naming, capitalization) require explicit documentation to ensure consistency across website and marketing materials
Payment option ordering should reflect audience preferences and demographics (e.g., check-first for rural, older populations)
Local SEO location pages require keyword research per geographic area and content tailored to local search intent
Copy drafting by the agency team (rather than waiting for client input) prompts faster editorial feedback and unblocks development
Zigzag layout (alternating image and content blocks) is used as a standard site design template for consistency across multiple client sites
Testimonials and social proof sections are being replaced or supplemented with visual product galleries and curated logo walls
Website performance issues (502 errors, slow load times, high query counts) are frequently caused by accumulated log files, bot traffic, or plugin conflicts
Unexplained or unauthorized CMS edits pose a risk when multiple stakeholders or external contractors retain lingering access to the site
Two-phase website launches (siloed launch followed by full redirect) are used to avoid disrupting existing user workflows during platform transitions
Manufacturer warranty authorization is a key differentiator for commercial roofing clients and should be prominently featured in messaging
Certification and affiliation logos are consolidated into scrolling carousels to avoid scattered, confusing layouts
Form-to-CRM integration failures are a recurring issue that can result in lost leads if forms are embedded on newly launched pages without proper wiring
Webflow's platform constraints limit direct server configuration, requiring workarounds via Cloudflare or custom code injection for security headers
Clients struggle with copy-to-layout mismatch: content written in documents often needs revision once placed in actual design
Service-based businesses benefit from consolidating product variants into single categories and handling sizing/configuration in the consultation process
Therapy and healthcare practices require HIPAA-compliant alternatives to standard contact forms, often routing primary CTAs to EHR booking systems instead
Static PDF/Canva catalogs create distribution friction and zero SEO value; migrating to live website sections with individual pages per item unlocks organic search
Separate WordPress domains are acceptable for campaign landing pages when traffic is driven by email/LinkedIn rather than organic search or PPC
Homepage design should answer four core questions on a single page: Who are we? What do we do? Who do we work with? Why trust us?
Clients benefit from structured content workflows (Word docs per page) that give them a clear task while providing developers a single source of truth
Multiple template variants (A/B/C) for specialty pages provide visual variety and layout flexibility without locking clients into a single structure
Landing page conversion optimization is critical when ad campaigns generate clicks but low form submission rates
B2B websites need to lead with results and specific value propositions rather than aesthetics alone, especially for ABM readiness
Mobile responsiveness issues (cropped images, layout overflow, text sizing) appear across multiple client sites and require systematic testing
CTAs are either vague, absent, or routed to wrong destinations (homepages instead of landing pages), creating conversion bottlenecks
Form submission failures occur silently, with users unaware that notifications failed to send or confirmations didn't display
Service pages with variable pricing (by vehicle, by configuration, by market) should route to product/shop pages rather than booking flows
Accessibility issues (low contrast, small text) are flagged as 'close to the edge' and deferred despite failing thresholds
Navigation labels optimized for SEO ('Dementia Care') confuse users; user-friendly labels work better when keyword-rich pages exist
High-traffic pages with zero or near-zero conversions indicate performance or UX issues that require immediate investigation
Sticky headers and navigation elements have z-index conflicts with decorative section dividers, causing navigation to disappear mid-page
Inclusive language updates (gender-neutral pronouns, role terminology) are being implemented to accommodate underrepresented market segments
Interactive tools (questionnaires, wizards, selectors) reduce friction in prospect qualification and staff workload
Placeholder images and outdated gallery content persist on live service pages, reducing credibility and conversion
Inactive legacy social accounts linked from website footers create brand confusion; cleanup requires both website link updates and account deletion
Website rebuilds are deferred until strategy sessions establish value proposition, positioning, and messaging direction
Location pages are built from templates with location-specific copy substitution to improve local SEO presence
Conversion audits reveal single forms across entire sites, missing CTAs, and generic messaging that doesn't articulate specific value
Website projects are blocked by missing client content or approvals rather than technical issues
Design teams tend to over-engineer when given open-ended briefs; constraining scope (e.g., 'simple Figma mocks only, 30-45 min per page') improves delivery
Contractors assigned multiple concurrent tasks struggle with prioritization; single-task assignment with explicit deadlines and mid-cycle check-ins improves delivery
Website content corrections require working sessions with clients to audit and standardize terminology across all pages
Hero image swaps and layout restructuring are common mid-project changes for page redesigns
Local SEO expansion relies on location page builds and footer address/map updates
Landing pages with single, prominent CTA outperform pages with multiple or generic CTAs
Vertical-specific messaging outperforms generic messaging for B2B audiences
Website content organization issues (mixed content types, broken links, garbled copy) go undetected without proper QA
Form field clarity improves conversion by reducing user confusion
Post-form submission clarity increases form completion rates
Templated, location-specific pages at scale capture geographic search variants
Content restructuring (separating blog, news, resources) prevents editorial confusion
Asymmetric uses hybrid flat + per-page maintenance contracts for variable-demand projects
WordPress is preferred for straightforward marketing sites; React/Supabase for complex custom features
Facility-specific content (packing lists, resources) must be authored per location due to variation
Printable formatting on resource pages increases utility for families/users
Local service businesses competing with similarly-named competitors need address visibility as conversion-critical element
Website footer is standard, expected location for business contact and location information
patterns.principle: appears on every page regardless of entry point

patterns.pattern: NAP (Name, Address, Phone) consistency across website and Google Business Profile matters for local SEO
State-level service area pages require standardization before scaling to remaining pages
City-level pages can extend state page structure to capture hyper-local search traffic
Campaign landing pages benefit from structured section sequence with specific content types
Specific metrics and examples land better than general statements in fundraising/impact messaging
patterns.example: 'Sarah's physical therapy progress stalls each winter' vs. generic 'seasonal closures'

patterns.pattern: Off-brand embedded forms create disjointed UX at critical conversion points
patterns.solution: redesign to match site branding while retaining underlying routing logic

patterns.pattern: Careers pages need updated benefits lists and standing job descriptions to attract talent
Navigation items that no longer serve page purpose should be removed
WordPress sites accumulate plugin conflicts and reliability issues over time
Content publishing friction in WordPress (write → optimize → export → manually build) can be streamlined with Next.js
patterns.improvement: single command from doc to published page

patterns.pattern: SEO maintenance tasks (meta descriptions, alt text, title tags) are dramatically faster in code-based frameworks
Named client testimonials on homepage create competitive vulnerability
patterns.evidence: Competitor used published testimonials to prospect and win away clients

patterns.pattern: Anonymous client quotes on project-level pages preserve social proof without creating prospecting list
patterns.applies_to: relationship-driven B2B (custom fabrication, luxury construction, bespoke manufacturing)

patterns.pattern: Inline form success messages are consistently misinterpreted as failures by users
patterns.user_perception: page 'popped back up' or failed to process

patterns.pattern: Dedicated Thank You page redirect provides unambiguous confirmation signal
Product page hero images with rendering issues (disappearing, artifacts) indicate Divi/WordPress build problems
Client feedback should be compiled top-to-bottom in single document rather than piecemeal
Facility/infrastructure projects benefit from construction progress logs with photos every 2-3 weeks
patterns.engagement: keeps stakeholders informed, supports transparency narrative

patterns.pattern: Facility pages should educate public on who benefits (not just the primary business)
patterns.context: FarmWise serves four-state region, not just Doudlah Farms

patterns.pattern: Homepage redesigns require both content replacement and structural reorganization
patterns.scope: copy, section order, CTA buttons, branding updates

patterns.pattern: Sticky header logo sizing should fill available space evenly
Footer copyright text should be centered under logo for visual balance
Mobile responsiveness bugs (hero overflow, icon stacking, button misalignment) block production launch
QR code strategy decision hinges on destination (manufacturer vs. custom pages), which drives cost/timeline/value
patterns.decision_framework: budget, timeline, SEO goals, content control

patterns.pattern: Product page launches should be delayed until all prerequisites (spec sheet, certificates, dedicated page) are ready
patterns.principle: press release links to complete experience, not homepage

patterns.pattern: Product page structure should follow consistent layout pattern across product catalog
Website copy should be SEO-optimized with FAQs, meta titles, and meta descriptions included
patterns.deliverables: body copy, FAQs, meta title, meta description per page

patterns.pattern: Image sourcing from limited social media presence (Instagram-only) requires high-resolution landscape orientation
React/Cursor/Vercel/Supabase stack is appropriate for custom applications with complex logic or data handling
Backend optimization work (security, performance, SEO) is invisible to clients but demonstrates proactive stewardship
Website health metrics span security (SSL, headers), performance (database size, caching), and SEO (meta descriptions, alt text)
Payment integration migrations require coordinating plugin removal, new integration setup, and end-to-end testing
patterns.urgency: active sponsor trying to complete transaction

patterns.pattern: Website errors and payment migrations may be related and could be addressed together in single technical pass
Navigation and information architecture refinements are often deprioritized relative to content/page build work, creating downstream dependencies
Landing pages for paid ads should mirror existing site structure but be optimized for specific keywords and audience segments rather than generic homepages
Form friction vs. lead quality is a recurring tension; shorter forms with post-submission qualification is preferred over long forms that suppress volume
Regional/geographic specificity in website copy and imagery is important for locally-focused businesses; generic or mismatched visuals signal inauthenticity
Site replacements over existing live domains require formal pre-launch planning (URL mapping, redirect setup, automation audit) to avoid post-launch cleanup
Outdated or overused project imagery should be retired in favor of newer work (5 years old or less preferred) to maintain freshness and relevance
Website QC (typos, broken links, incorrect data) is often user-flagged rather than caught internally; parallel QC passes by domain experts and copyeditors improve catch rate
Hosting migrations should be timed to avoid subscription renewal dates; free hosting during project transition can facilitate move to managed retainer model
Interactive tools (wizards, questionnaires, filters) can refresh website experience without requiring full redesign
Warranty and certification messaging should be simplified to avoid over-explaining unless it is a genuine competitive differentiator
Leadership/team pages require careful photo management and role clarity (staff vs. board) to avoid confusion and accommodate personnel changes
Website changes without formal approval process lead to unapproved edits, wrong contact information, and truncated copy going live
Client content delays block near-complete website launches, creating financial urgency (hosting fees accruing)
Website launches require explicit DNS migration planning with hosting overlap to prevent downtime
B2B website projects require explicit timeline updates and clear design-to-development handoff sequences
Landing pages built from AI-generated concepts require account manager alignment before design work begins
Website updates often surface multiple content maintenance issues (outdated images, stale service listings, inconsistent language) during review cycles
Structured meeting cadences (twice-weekly) accelerate website development progress compared to email-only coordination
Vertical-specific landing pages outperform generic pages for cold outreach campaigns
Template-based page builds reduce turnaround time when full custom design is impractical
Website redesigns and refreshes require prior concept assets (sitemaps, mockups, user flows) to accelerate planning
Annual reports pages benefit from tile-based layouts that accommodate both PDF and video formats
Client control and direct access to website management is a critical pain point driving platform migrations
Dedicated landing pages and specialized pages outperform generic/homepage destinations across multiple contexts
Interactive tools (wizards, questionnaires, calculators) reduce friction and improve lead quality in senior living and healthcare contexts
Homepage simplification and menu parity improves user orientation and reduces bounce rates
Client-supplied assets (videos, logos, photos) are frequently delayed or require rework before launch
Staging review and client sign-off process is critical to preventing last-minute launch delays
Messaging pillars and value propositions must differentiate from larger competitors and address buyer pain points
Post-launch functionality testing (forms, payment integrations) often reveals issues only after go-live
Navigation label clarity improves user comprehension and reduces internal jargon in public-facing copy
Full website rebuilds require careful URL preservation strategy to maintain SEO equity
Microsite and redirect strategies allow sub-brands to grow while preserving main domain authority and trademark protection
Product-specific page optimization requires addressing pricing display, photography, and cross-linking gaps
Multiple clients (VCEDC, American Extractions, Bluepoint, A New Dawn Therapy) require content restructuring or clarification to better serve user intent and conversion goals
Pre-launch review processes identify multiple small but important fixes (typos, imagery, missing content) that should be caught before go-live
Placeholder or generic content (lorem ipsum, test copy, vague CTAs) damages credibility and indicates inadequate pre-launch QA
Landing pages with strong ad engagement but low conversion rates indicate messaging or value proposition issues rather than targeting problems
Asymmetric standardizes on specific tools across clients: Gravity Forms for forms, approved SEO plugin, Google Fonts, Vimeo for video hosting
Clients with external vendor dependencies (Cureline, Quarra Italia) benefit from clear ownership models or full control to enable faster iteration
Design decisions benefit from multiple rounds of iteration and client feedback before finalization (VCEDC header color, Bluepoint CTAs, American Extractions product structure)

contradictions.claim_a: Thin, elegant fonts match logos and should be used for brand consistency
contradictions.claim_b: Thin, elegant fonts do not translate well to body copy at smaller sizes and bold fonts should be prioritized for readability
contradictions.resolution: Readability over elegance is the approved principle; client sign-off required when deviating from logo font

contradictions.claim_a: Open sections (permanently visible) surface emotional content immediately and help users self-identify as candidates
contradictions.claim_b: Permanently open sections create excessive scroll depth on mobile, reduce attention span, and create visual noise; accordions are the standard pattern
contradictions.resolution: Deferred to heat map data and actual user behavior analytics rather than opinion

contradictions.claim_a: Pricing should be displayed prominently inline to set expectations early
contradictions.claim_b: Pricing should be hidden behind an accordion to avoid sticker shock and allow users to understand value before seeing cost
contradictions.resolution: Accordion approach approved; users who care about price will seek it out

contradictions.claim_a: Complimentary Elementor rebuild includes building pages that already have a defined design or template
contradictions.claim_b: Designing new page layouts for pages that lack an existing template is outside the scope of complimentary rebuild
contradictions.note: Not a true contradiction — these are complementary statements defining scope boundaries. Included for clarity on what is/is not covered.

contradictions.claim_a: Testimonials should be displayed on the homepage to build trust
contradictions.claim_b: Testimonials are being replaced with visual product galleries to serve as implicit social proof without requiring written quotes
contradictions.context: Different client strategies; Quaritalia is proceeding with testimonials (pending competitive sensitivity review), while PaperTube is replacing them with visual galleries

contradictions.claim_a: Homepage copy should be expanded to meet SEO word count targets (300–500+ words minimum)
contradictions.claim_b: Homepage copy that is expanded for SEO purposes often reads as overwhelming in actual page layout and should be distributed across sections rather than dense paragraphs
contradictions.note: Not a true contradiction — the same source resolves this by recommending word count targets be met through distributed sections (FAQs, feature lists, cross-links) rather than dense paragraphs. Included for clarity on the tension.

contradictions.claim_a: Two-column project grid layouts maximize visual impact of photography
contradictions.claim_b: Mobile rendering was a secondary consideration supporting the two-column choice
contradictions.note: Not a direct contradiction, but tension: two-column layout was chosen for visual impact, yet mobile rendering was secondary consideration. Unclear if mobile experience was optimized or compromised.

contradictions.claim_a: The Divi Builder has been less smooth than other builders used on other projects
contradictions.claim_b: No explicit comparison of builder performance across other clients in the batch
contradictions.note: Single mention of Divi performance issue without comparative data from other builders on other projects.

contradictions.claim_a: Gravity Forms is standard for WordPress form collection
contradictions.claim_b: HIPAA compliance is not guaranteed out of the box with Gravity Forms
contradictions.note: Not a direct contradiction but a caveat: Gravity Forms is standard but requires compliance verification per client

contradictions.claim_a: BluePoint ATM state pages are ready to be added to site navigation
contradictions.claim_b: Several issues must be resolved before remaining 40 pages are built out and section is added to navigation
contradictions.resolution: No contradiction — article states issues must be resolved BEFORE adding to navigation; sequencing is clear

contradictions.claim_a: Inline form success messages work correctly (functioning as intended)
contradictions.claim_b: Inline form success messages cause user confusion and are frequently reported as bugs
contradictions.resolution: No contradiction — article distinguishes between technical functionality and user perception; both are true

contradictions.claim_a: Reducing form friction (shorter forms) leads to more conversions but lower-quality leads, requiring post-submission qualification
contradictions.claim_b: Longer forms with more fields improve lead quality by filtering at the form level
contradictions.note: Not a contradiction between sources, but an acknowledged tension within the same source; the recommendation is to favor shorter forms + post-submission qualification

contradictions.claim_a: Phone numbers should be prominently visible on all interior pages to reduce friction for phone-preferring users
contradictions.claim_b: State pages should display only the local CallRail number in the hero and move the generic 800 number to footer-only to avoid confusion
contradictions.note: Not a true contradiction; the resolution is a hierarchy where local numbers take precedence on state pages while generic numbers remain visible site-wide via the shared template bar

contradictions.claim_a: WordPress is the standard platform for website builds
contradictions.source_a: Multiple articles reference WordPress as default
contradictions.claim_b: JavaScript rebuilds are faster, lighter weight, and eliminate plugin management issues compared to WordPress
contradictions.note: Not a direct contradiction but represents a strategic shift in platform choice for certain project types

contradictions.claim_a: Generic CTAs like 'Learn More' are insufficient for bottom-of-funnel paid ad traffic
contradictions.claim_b: The American Extractions overview page uses 'Learn More' CTAs to link to individual product pages, which is the current structure being maintained
contradictions.note: Not a direct contradiction, but tension: Bluepoint recommends replacing 'Learn More' while American Extractions article does not flag this as a problem on their overview page.

exceptions.general_rule: Platform migrations should be avoided to preserve SEO equity
exceptions.exception: Seamless Building Systems must perform a full rebuild from proprietary Haibu platform to WordPress because no direct import path exists
exceptions.context: Non-WordPress proprietary CMS with no standard migration tools available

exceptions.general_rule: Contact Us pages should be built early as highest priority
exceptions.exception: PaperTube's WordPress/Shopify hybrid model defers some contact/lead routing decisions pending A/B testing and client internal consultation
exceptions.context: Structural platform change with dual audience segments requires sequencing and client buy-in before full implementation

exceptions.general_rule: Website projects should proceed on active timeline with regular client engagement
exceptions.exception: Shine website is intentionally paused on active traffic generation while foundational infrastructure is built during client's low-availability period
exceptions.context: Client (Katie Geiser) has limited bandwidth due to personal commitments and focus on other client (A New Dawn Therapy)

exceptions.general_rule: Sold-out inventory should be hidden from public-facing pages
exceptions.exception: Didion golf sponsorship tiers could optionally remain visible with 'Sold Out' label to show what was available
exceptions.context: Transparency about availability vs. clean UX; preferred approach is to hide entirely

exceptions.general_rule: Maps should only appear on Contact Us pages
exceptions.exception: Maps may be embedded in footers or other pages if contextually relevant, but this is discouraged due to page weight
exceptions.context: General best practice with acknowledged exceptions

exceptions.general_rule: Asymmetric should avoid editing websites it does not manage
exceptions.exception: Targeted landing pages can be created for ad-specific traffic without taking over the existing site
exceptions.context: When full website management is not feasible or desired by client

exceptions.general_rule: SSL certificate warnings deter all visitors
exceptions.exception: In the Anthemium case, the security warning caused an inbound inquiry to be suspected as a scam, but the inquiry still came through
exceptions.context: High-intent inbound leads may persist despite security warnings, but trust is damaged

exceptions.general_rule: Divi theme pages can be edited through the visual editor
exceptions.exception: Custom Slick Slider elements injected via functions.php cannot be edited through Divi's visual editor; they require direct code edits
exceptions.context: Custom-coded elements that bypass Divi's native architecture

exceptions.general_rule: Fieldwell should distance itself from American Extractions branding
exceptions.exception: When referencing the specific extraction technology/process, American Extractions trademark should be acknowledged

exceptions.general_rule: Project content should be drafted by the agency team to prompt faster editorial feedback
exceptions.exception: Project pages are an exception — project content (descriptions, bios, imagery) must come directly from the client team and cannot be drafted by the agency

exceptions.general_rule: All landing page navigation should be removed to prevent prospects from clicking away before converting
exceptions.exception: A Contact Us link is retained as the only navigation element, which anchor-scrolls down to the lead form at the bottom of the page

exceptions.general_rule: Warranty language should be consistent across all service pages
exceptions.exception: Repair and maintenance pages should have all warranty mentions removed because repairs are often short-term fixes and warranties vary by job

exceptions.general_rule: Manufacturer warranty authorization should be prominently featured for all roofing services
exceptions.exception: Roof inspection pages do not require warranty language because inspections are not a warrantied service

exceptions.general_rule: All managed sites should maintain an Ahrefs health score of 80+
exceptions.exception: Multiple sites were found scoring in the teens (Digital Arms B2B: 11, Three Gates: 16), indicating systemic backend problems requiring prioritized remediation

exceptions.general_rule: Separate domains reduce SEO authority and hurt PPC performance
exceptions.exception: Separate WordPress domains for campaign landing pages carry no meaningful downside when traffic is driven entirely by email and LinkedIn (not PPC or organic search)

exceptions.general_rule: Client imagery throughout a website creates maintenance burden when relationships end
exceptions.exception: Client logos in a logo banner are acceptable; this is lower-stakes than featuring client imagery throughout the page

exceptions.general_rule: Contact forms should be the primary conversion action on websites
exceptions.exception: Therapy and healthcare practices should route primary CTAs to EHR booking systems instead of contact forms to ensure HIPAA compliance

exceptions.general_rule: Product variants should be presented as distinct listings to allow customer self-selection
exceptions.exception: Service-based e-commerce sites should consolidate variants into single categories and handle sizing/configuration in the consultation process when customers lack the knowledge to self-select
exceptions.context: Service-based businesses where customers don't know technical specifications

exceptions.general_rule: Service pages should route to booking flows
exceptions.exception: Service pages with variable pricing (LED headlights: $300–$350 installed, varies by vehicle) should route to shop/product pages instead

exceptions.general_rule: SEO-driven navigation labels should be avoided in favor of user-friendly labels
exceptions.exception: SEO value can be preserved by ensuring a dedicated, keyword-rich page exists even if the nav label is user-friendly

exceptions.general_rule: All resource logos should be displayed at equal size to avoid hierarchy
exceptions.exception: Position (top-left, top-right) can signal priority without creating visual size hierarchy
exceptions.context: partner organization websites with multiple stakeholders

exceptions.general_rule: Forms should live on WordPress with Gravity Forms
exceptions.exception: If client adopts a CRM platform with capable native forms (HubSpot, Jane App, Go High Level), forms should live in the CRM instead

exceptions.general_rule: Accessibility issues should be fixed immediately
exceptions.exception: Low contrast and small text issues flagged as 'close to the edge' are sometimes deferred despite failing thresholds

exceptions.general_rule: Website launches should be blocked until all dependencies are resolved
exceptions.exception: AAG continued development work on Quaritalia site in parallel with unresolved invoice and missing content, proceeding in good faith
exceptions.context: Client payment and content delivery blockers

exceptions.general_rule: Experimental or custom builds should be avoided when launch deadlines are active
exceptions.exception: Asymmetric team initially explored custom JavaScript build despite active launch blockers

exceptions.general_rule: Landing pages should minimize navigation and off-page distractions
exceptions.exception: Standalone landing pages may need to decide whether to include main site footer or simplified footer

exceptions.general_rule: Contact forms should collect information before visitor is convinced of value
exceptions.exception: Service businesses with strong organic traffic can receive inquiries via footer email even without dedicated contact page
exceptions.context: A New Dawn Therapy received 3+ inquiries per week via footer email despite incomplete site

exceptions.general_rule: All ad traffic should route to dedicated landing pages
exceptions.exception: Homepage may be acceptable destination if it is conversion-optimized and matches ad intent
exceptions.context: Aviary's homepage was not conversion-focused, making it unsuitable

exceptions.general_rule: Sticky header language selector should support multiple languages
exceptions.exception: Dropdown format allows future expansion without redesign, but initial implementation may start with 2-3 languages

exceptions.general_rule: Testimonials on website homepages provide social proof
exceptions.exception: Named client testimonials create competitive vulnerability in relationship-driven B2B
exceptions.context: Quarra Italia, custom fabrication, luxury construction, bespoke manufacturing
exceptions.workaround: Use anonymous quotes on project-level pages instead

exceptions.general_rule: Add to Calendar widgets work on Thank You pages
exceptions.exception: Calendar plugins that pull event data from originating page cannot access that data on separate Thank You page

exceptions.general_rule: WordPress is suitable for most marketing sites
exceptions.exception: WordPress struggles with custom booking logic, complex B2B portals, multi-step user flows, enterprise data handling
exceptions.context: Projects requiring unusual feature requirements or high customization

exceptions.general_rule: Alt text coverage should approach 100%
exceptions.exception: Non-image file types (PDFs, documents) cannot have alt text applied; practical ceiling is ~89-90%

exceptions.general_rule: January is a slow period for garage door service
exceptions.exception: Complete cessation of form submissions (not gradual decline) warrants technical investigation
exceptions.principle: healthy baseline should still produce occasional submissions in slow months

exceptions.general_rule: All website changes should go through formal approval before going live
exceptions.exception: No exception documented; the two-step approval process is presented as mandatory without carve-outs

exceptions.general_rule: Dedicated landing pages should not be linked from main site navigation to keep traffic attribution clean
exceptions.exception: Ad-specific landing pages are hosted on the client domain but not discoverable via main navigation; they are only accessible via direct ad links

exceptions.general_rule: Stock photography should reflect the client's local geography
exceptions.exception: When client project photos are not available, generic Midwest-appropriate stock is acceptable as a fallback

exceptions.general_rule: Blog category filters should use URL parameters or anchors to enable deep-linking to filtered views
exceptions.exception: If the native CMS does not support filtered URL deep-linking out of the box, a developer must implement custom functionality
exceptions.context: A New Dawn Book Club blog filter

exceptions.general_rule: Website development capacity is not yet generating revenue
exceptions.exception: Raphael is confirmed to continue leading website work with design team support, indicating active ongoing projects

exceptions.general_rule: New website builds require SEO setup work (meta tags, technical configuration)
exceptions.exception: This work was previously handled by Yeshwant, whose role was eliminated, creating a gap in the process

exceptions.general_rule: Landing pages should be built on the main site where possible
exceptions.exception: Asymmetric landing pages were built in HubSpot (not JavaScript) to prioritize speed over theme-level integration
exceptions.context: Temporary workaround for urgent email campaign launches

exceptions.general_rule: Multi-product navigation should be avoided to prevent diluting core message
exceptions.exception: Safety and Security page must remain as a top-level navigation item despite multi-product strategy, because it is a trust signal actively sought by credit union buyers

exceptions.general_rule: Homepages should not be used as ad landing pages
exceptions.exception: Repurposing existing high-quality pages (e.g., recently built dealer pages) is valid when full landing page build isn't feasible

exceptions.general_rule: Avoid clever/creative headlines that omit keywords
exceptions.exception: Not explicitly stated, but implies creative headlines may be acceptable in non-SEO contexts

exceptions.general_rule: Microsite strategy requires dense cross-linking to main site
exceptions.exception: Full redirect (301) was considered but rejected for Quarra Italia because European visitor landing on USA site may perceive it as irrelevant

exceptions.general_rule: Client-initiated action on landing pages rarely produces results
exceptions.exception: Draft pages internally and present near-finished version for sign-off rather than waiting for client approval

exceptions.general_rule: All embedded YouTube videos should have branding suppressed for clean design
exceptions.exception: Snowmobile photo on VCEDC 'Living in Vilas County' page was retained despite Kathy's mild ambivalence about it, because it serves the economic development mission

exceptions.general_rule: Product pages should contain more detailed content than overview pages to reward deeper navigation
exceptions.exception: American Extractions' 'Numerous Possibilities' section treatment varies by product: kept for Stick Packs and Gummies, likely removed for Tinctures, repurposed for ClearMix

exceptions.general_rule: Landing pages should have a single tracked phone number to ensure proper attribution
exceptions.exception: Bluepoint landing pages had three separate phone numbers in footer, none confirmed as tracked
exceptions.context: Bluepoint ATM landing pages (identified as problem, not best practice)

evidence.metric: Domain Rating 53 with 858 referring domains
evidence.context: Asymmetric Marketing website; justifies staying on Beaver Builder rather than migrating to Elementor

evidence.metric: 227 organic keywords, ~5,900 monthly visitors
evidence.context: Asymmetric site organic traffic; declining but recoverable with content refresh

evidence.metric: 100+ blog posts in Beaver Builder
evidence.context: Content volume that would be lost or require manual re-entry if migrating to Elementor

evidence.metric: ~90% of Overhead Door web form traffic is residential
evidence.context: Justifies prioritizing residential CTA form improvements

evidence.metric: 202 monthly visits yielding only ~6 engagements
evidence.context: Seamless website pre-redesign performance; target is 3 legitimate leads per month

evidence.metric: 42 case studies on Seamless website
evidence.context: Content scope for WordPress migration; top 20 prioritized for prominent placement

evidence.metric: 5 service pages requiring minimum 1,000 words each for SEO
evidence.context: Content requirements for Seamless website rebuild

evidence.metric: 30-day cancellation notice required for Haibu hosting
evidence.context: Timing constraint for Seamless platform migration; must not cancel until WordPress site is live

evidence.metric: Security score 0/100, target 90+; Page Speed 45/100, target 90+; ADA Accessibility none, target compliant
evidence.context: The Cordwainer website baseline metrics; critical fixes required within first week

evidence.metric: February 17, 2026 go-live date for FinWellU
evidence.context: Launch timeline gated on client copy delivery by Feb 2 EOD

evidence.metric: January–February 2026 rebuild window for Citrus America
evidence.context: Deliberately timed during slower sales months to concentrate ~75% of AAG effort

evidence.metric: Minimum 2 posts/month recommended cadence for FinWellU blog
evidence.context: Post-launch Phase 2 content production under retainer

evidence.metric: American Extractions website launched successfully in October 2025 with brief 1-2 hour outage during post-launch update
evidence.context: Site migration and launch

evidence.metric: Meta descriptions were not carried over during American Extractions site migration and required manual addition post-launch
evidence.context: SEO metadata gap during migration

evidence.metric: SBSWI website redesign targets mid-February launch with dropdown navigation for 6 service categories
evidence.context: Information architecture redesign

evidence.metric: Cordwainer staff photos had heads cut off and faces obscured by gray overlays on multiple pages
evidence.context: Hero image UX issue

evidence.metric: Cordwainer 'Importance of Lighting' blog post was getting traffic but likely losing readers due to unbroken text walls

evidence.metric: Cordwainer 'Schedule a Visit' information page had no link or button to actually schedule a visit
evidence.context: Missing CTA on conversion-intent page

evidence.metric: Quarra Stone homepage hero currently displays rotating slideshow of finished project photos
evidence.context: Current state before video replacement

evidence.metric: Didion website had duplicate 'Corn Masa Flour' page that predated the final 'Culinary Collection' page

evidence.metric: Didion Culinary Collection page had Slick Slider rendering twice (once from custom code, once from Divi row)
evidence.context: Duplicate element issue with custom code

evidence.metric: Shine Marketing contact forms are not delivering to client's email inbox despite identical setup to working A New Dawn Therapy site
evidence.context: Email delivery failure
evidence.client: Shine Marketing / A New Dawn Therapy

evidence.metric: Adava Care QR code at Glendale community is non-functional while QR codes at all other communities are active
evidence.context: Technical issue with specific location asset

evidence.metric: VCEDC donate page presents three payment options in order: Mail a Check, PayPal, Stripe
evidence.context: Payment option ordering for rural audience

evidence.metric: Cordwainer gallery page missing 2-minute facility video, has wrong hero photo, and photos incorrectly sorted under subheadings
evidence.context: Multiple content/layout issues on single page

evidence.metric: Cordwainer events page flyer images cropped on mobile, hiding headers and faces
evidence.context: Mobile display bug

evidence.metric: Cordwainer leadership page bio text cut off on mobile (first two lines not visible)
evidence.context: Mobile layout issue

evidence.metric: AHS 'Request a Training' page implied it was only for employers with staff; needed messaging update to include non-employer organizations
evidence.context: Audience clarity issue

evidence.metric: AHS form lacked address capture for off-site training requests; conditional address field was needed
evidence.context: Form logic gap

evidence.metric: Blue Sky Capital project approved at $1,500 including press release, Google Ads strategy, and website audit
evidence.context: Project scope and budget

evidence.metric: Quarra Design Assist page rename from 'Stone Sourcing Services' has negligible SEO impact (neither phrase has high search volume)

evidence.metric: BluepointATM calculator demo is available for review at bluepointcalculators.com

evidence.metric: Avant Gardening expressed strong interest in website optimization but needed to evaluate cost feasibility before committing (January 2026)

evidence.metric: Site size reduced from 380 MB to 66 MB; page load time improved from 6s+ to under 2s; database queries reduced from ~900 to under 200 per page load
evidence.context: After server cleanup removing accumulated log files and bot traffic

evidence.metric: Ahrefs score improved from ~40 → 80 after backend fixes
evidence.context: Website health remediation

evidence.metric: Multiple sites scoring in the teens (Digital Arms B2B: 11, Three Gates: 16)

evidence.metric: 17 Google AI Overview citations; 0 ChatGPT citations
evidence.context: AI citation baseline for website

evidence.metric: 99% Ahrefs health score; A+ security rating

evidence.metric: Organic keywords doubled; organic traffic up ~40% to 1,600 visitors/month
evidence.context: After launching state pages as part of content expansion strategy

evidence.metric: 10 state pages launched and live on interactive map; second batch of 10 drafted and pending client review
evidence.context: Service Coverage section with state-specific landing pages

evidence.metric: ~5 organic visits/month; only 2 indexed keywords
evidence.context: Current organic performance before security header remediation

evidence.metric: Healthy database query range: 50–100 queries per page load; scores of 600+ indicate serious problems
evidence.context: Query Monitor diagnostic benchmark

evidence.metric: ~2,200 organic visits/month; 174 keywords ranking in positions 1–3; 182 in positions 4–10
evidence.context: Paper Tube Co website SEO health assessment

evidence.context: Social proof inventory for B2B company

evidence.metric: Only one or two inbound contact form submissions over prior month
evidence.context: Lead generation from Meta and Google ad campaigns despite decent click volume

evidence.metric: Target cost per result approximately $1.00
evidence.context: Service business ad efficiency benchmark

evidence.metric: 5k–10k impression threshold needed for statistical significance before landing page optimization
evidence.context: Campaign maturity required before CRO work

evidence.metric: Two Google Forms created for October 15th training sessions (Contractors and Realtors)
evidence.context: Immediate registration mechanism while full training page is being built

evidence.metric: 10 state pages live with plans to expand to 50
evidence.context: BluepointATM state-specific service pages expansion

evidence.metric: Hero image crops ATM unit on mobile viewports
evidence.context: mobile responsiveness issue on state pages

evidence.metric: Phone number appears only in hero image with no secondary placement
evidence.context: call conversion bottleneck on state pages

evidence.metric: Wizard in feedback and review phase with positive feedback from CEO and admissions team pending
evidence.context: interactive questionnaire tool development status

evidence.metric: Approximately three questions per branch to avoid drop-off
evidence.context: questionnaire design principle

evidence.metric: All phone-screen layout issues resolved via Playwright automated testing
evidence.context: mobile responsiveness validation

evidence.metric: Light purple text on champagne background fails contrast thresholds for low vision users
evidence.context: accessibility issue flagged

evidence.metric: Gender-neutral language accommodates ~20% of wedding market (same-sex and non-binary couples)
evidence.context: inclusive language update rationale

evidence.metric: Two-column project grid layout chosen over three columns for visual impact

evidence.metric: Five planned languages: English, Italian, French, German, Spanish
evidence.context: multilingual support scope

evidence.metric: Navigation label 'Dementia Care' was confusing; changed to 'Living & Care'
evidence.context: navigation overhaul to improve user clarity

evidence.metric: Mobile conversion score of 80 on deck page due to slow loading

evidence.metric: All conversions for Exterior Renovations currently coming from mobile devices
evidence.context: mobile-first conversion pattern

evidence.metric: Sticky header disappears when scrolling past slanted section dividers

evidence.metric: Dynamic logo resize on scroll implemented as reference pattern
